stories.title: Andy Burnham becomes UK's new Prime Minister, replacing Keir Starmer
stories.summary: Andy Burnham entered No. 10 on 20 July 2026 as the UK's seventh Prime Minister in a decade, after Keir Starmer formally resigned to the King at Buckingham Palace. Burnham, former Mayor of Greater Manchester, pledged to be a 'circuit breaker' and promised a new economic model.

stories.title: Starmer leaves No. 10 with 'good grace and a smile'
stories.summary: Keir Starmer gave a final speech from Downing Street praising Britons' 'resolve, grit, decency and compassion', saying Britain is 'stronger and fairer than it was 2 years ago'. He then formally resigned to the King before Burnham took the keys to No. 10.

stories.title: Burnham pledges to end rough sleeping and deliver cost of living support
stories.summary: In his first words as PM, Burnham said his first instruction would be to 'end rough sleeping in our country', and pledged to make announcements this week to 'give people some breathing space' on the cost of living.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, Lords, PMQs & Legislation

stories.title: Burnham returned to Parliament via Makerfield by-election ahead of Labour leadership win
stories.summary: Andy Burnham had been parachuted back into the House of Commons via the Makerfield special election in June 2026 in order to pursue the Labour leadership, having previously served as Mayor of Greater Manchester.

stories.title: New Parliament session awaits under Burnham government
stories.summary: With Burnham's formation of a new cabinet, Parliament is expected to reconvene with a fresh legislative agenda focused on economic reform, devolution, and cost of living measures.

stories.title: Who's in Andy Burnham's new Labour cabinet?
stories.summary: Burnham appointed John Healey as Chancellor, Ed Miliband as Foreign Secretary, Yvette Cooper as Health Secretary, Angela Rayner (back) as Housing Secretary, Wes Streeting as Defence Secretary, and Shabana Mahmood stays as Home Secretary. Starmer ally Darren Jones was sacked.

stories.title: Burnham's policy choices reveal what kind of PM he wants to be
stories.summary: Analysis suggests Burnham's cabinet picks and early policy signals — ending rough sleeping, cost of living support, and devolution pledges — indicate a populist-left, community-focused agenda distinct from the Starmer era.

stories.title: Burnham to set out cost of living support this week
stories.summary: The new PM has promised to announce cost of living measures this week to 'give people some breathing space', with Healey as chancellor expected to outline financial support.

stories.title: Kemi Badenoch congratulates Burnham and offers to work with him on benefits cuts
stories.summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch wrote to Burnham wishing him success, and said Conservatives would vote with the government on benefits bill savings if agreement is reached. She had previously mocked Burnham as 'a pair of eyelashes and a black T-shirt'.

stories.title: Burnham becomes UK's seventh PM in a decade — a challenge for opposition
stories.summary: With yet another change of leadership, the Conservatives face the challenge of holding a new PM to account as Burnham promises a 'circuit breaker' from recent political instability.

stories.title: Lib Dems: Burnham 'has a very short window to turn this government around'
stories.summary: The Liberal Democrats acknowledged Burnham's arrival as PM but warned he faces a tight window to deliver real change, saying the new PM must 'end the chaos' of recent years.

stories.title: Lib Dem Voice: 'Burnham has made a promising start — now comes the hard part'
stories.summary: Lib Dem commentator Mathew Hulbert wrote that while Burnham's first speech was encouraging, the hard work of governing begins now, with many uncosted policy priorities stacking up.

stories.title: Farage investigation over undeclared gifts branded 'kangaroo court' by Reform MP
stories.summary: Reform MP Sarah Pochin (Runcorn) defended Farage, calling the parliamentary investigation into millions of pounds in undeclared gifts a 'vicious and desperate campaign' by the establishment to stop Reform UK.

stories.title: Clacton by-election set for early August with 30+ candidates, major parties boycotting
stories.summary: Farage resigned his Clacton seat over the donations scandal, triggering a by-election in which he is re-contesting. All major parties have boycotted the contest. Candidates include Count Binface and an Australian, Daniel Pocock.

stories.title: YouGov poll: Reform voters split on Farage resignation — 57% support, 16% oppose
stories.summary: Among the general public, 24% support Farage's resignation and 43% oppose it. Among Reform voters, 57% support the move versus 16% who oppose, suggesting strong backing from his base for the by-election gamble.

stories.title: Douglas Alexander to remain Scottish Secretary under Burnham
stories.summary: Despite major cabinet changes, Douglas Alexander confirmed he is 'very happy' to stay on as Scottish Secretary under PM Burnham, providing continuity in Scotland's relationship with Westminster.

stories.title: Jack McConnell urges Scottish Labour to debate split from UK party
stories.summary: Former First Minister Lord McConnell said Scottish Labour should be allowed to debate different ideas, including greater autonomy from the UK party, as Burnham enters No. 10 promising devolution.

stories.title: Burnham pledges to 'carry power to every postcode'
stories.summary: New PM Andy Burnham promised in his first speech to build 'a national sense of unity' and devolve power across the UK — a signal that Scottish and regional interests will be central to his agenda.

stories.title: Healey made Chancellor, Miliband Foreign Secretary in Burnham's new cabinet
stories.summary: John Healey takes over as Chancellor and Ed Miliband becomes Foreign Secretary in a major reshaping of Labour's top team. The surprise pick of Healey as Chancellor — a political veteran — drew attention.

stories.title: Rayner returns as Housing Secretary; Streeting named Defence Secretary
stories.summary: Angela Rayner returns to government as Housing Secretary, while Wes Streeting takes on the Defence brief. Shabana Mahmood stays on as Home Secretary, providing some continuity.

stories.title: Lord Hermer departs as Attorney-General; Darren Jones sacked
stories.summary: Lord Hermer has left his role as Attorney-General, and Darren Jones — a close Starmer ally — was sacked from the new cabinet, signalling a clean break from the previous administration's inner circle.

stories.title: Clacton by-election: Farage re-contests seat vacated over donations scandal
stories.summary: Nigel Farage resigned his Clacton seat to force a by-election, asking voters to judge him directly over the undeclared gifts scandal. The contest in early August has attracted 30+ candidates but all major parties are boycotting.

stories.title: Count Binface and Australian Daniel Pocock among Clacton candidates
stories.summary: The Clacton by-election has drawn an eclectic field including Count Binface, three Monster Raving Loony Party candidates, and Melbourne-born Daniel Pocock, who says he has 'an equally good chance of winning'.

stories.title: Lord Hermer departs as Attorney-General with Burnham government
stories.summary: Lord Hermer has left his post as Attorney-General following the change of PM, with new appointments to the Lords-based legal roles expected as Burnham builds his ministerial team.

stories.title: Lord McConnell calls for Scottish Labour to debate greater autonomy
stories.summary: Former First Minister Lord McConnell used a BBC Radio Scotland interview to urge Scottish Labour to openly debate separating from the UK party structure, as Burnham's devolution agenda reshapes centre-left politics.
